title: pasta support for systemd network units
body:
### Feature request description

Currently the [documentation for network units](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html#driver-defaults-to-bridge) lists the supported network modes (documented as "drivers") as `bridge`, `macvlan`, and `ipvlan`. The [network mode documentation](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-run.1.html#network-mode-net) does not list `macvlan` or `ipvlan`, so presumably those are no longer options.

Without `pasta`, I do not see a path for systemd activated containers to see the real IP address of a request unless the container supports socket activation.

On a lark I did set up a network unit file with `Driver=pasta`, which predictably failed with "Error: failed to find driver or plugin "pasta"".

### Suggest potential solution

Make network units support the same network modes as the podman CLI.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body:
@nielsenb-jf as far as I know (and from a quick test) if systemd units are rootless/user units, the default network back-end is pasta.

The comment in podman-systemd.unit(5) looks misleading to me, as it only applies to containers running as root. Did you actually try running things as unprivileged user? Or you're trying to set up quadlets to run as root, and you would need pasta support for those?
